children.text: A fishery endorsement may be issued for a vessel that—

children.tail: and, if owned by an entity, the entity satisfies the ownership requirements in subsection (c);

children.text: was built in the United States;
if rebuilt, was rebuilt in the United States;
was not forfeited to the United States Government after

children.tail: , for a breach of the laws of the United States; and

children.text: otherwise qualifies under the laws of the United States to engage in the fisheries.
Subject to the laws of the United States regulating the fisheries, a vessel for which a fishery endorsement is issued may engage in the fisheries.

A fishery endorsement is invalid immediately if the vessel for which it is issued is used as a fishing vessel while it is chartered or leased to an individual who is not a citizen of the United States or to an entity that is not eligible to own a vessel with a fishery endorsement.

A vessel owned by an entity is eligible for a fishery endorsement only if at least 75 percent of the interest in the entity, at each tier of ownership and in the aggregate, is owned and controlled by citizens of the United States.
In determining whether at least 75 percent of the interest in the entity is owned and controlled by citizens of the United States under paragraph (1), the Secretary shall apply

children.tail: , except that for this purpose the terms “control” or “controlled”—

children.text: direct the business of the entity;
limit the actions of or replace the chief executive officer, a majority of the board of directors, any general partner, or any person serving in a management capacity of the entity; or
direct the transfer, operation, or manning of a vessel with a fishery endorsement; but

do not include the right to simply participate in the activities under subparagraph (A), or the exercise of rights under loan or mortgage covenants by a mortgagee eligible to be a preferred mortgagee under

children.tail: , except that a mortgagee not eligible to own a vessel with a fishery endorsement may only operate such a vessel to the extent necessary for the immediate safety of the vessel or for repairs, drydocking, or berthing changes.

children.text: This subsection does not apply to a vessel when it is engaged in the fisheries in the exclusive economic zone under the authority of the Western Pacific Fishery Management Council established under section 302(a)(1)(H) of the Magnuson-Stevens Fishery Conservation and Management Act (

children.tail: ) or to a purse seine vessel when it is engaged in tuna fishing in the Pacific Ocean outside the exclusive economic zone or pursuant to the South Pacific Regional Fisheries Treaty, provided that the owner of the vessel continues to comply with the eligibility requirements for a fishery endorsement under the Federal law that was in effect on
. A fishery endorsement issued pursuant to this paragraph is valid for engaging only in the activities described in this paragraph.

children.text: Requirements Based on Length, Tonnage, or Horsepower
This subsection applies to a vessel that—
is greater than 165 feet in registered length;
is more than 750 gross registered tons as measured under chapter 145 of this title or 1,900 gross registered tons as measured under chapter 143 of this title; or
has an engine or engines capable of producing a total of more than 3,000 shaft horsepower.
A vessel subject to this subsection is not eligible for a fishery endorsement unless—
a certificate of documentation was issued for the vessel and endorsed with a fishery endorsement that was effective on
the vessel is not placed under foreign registry after
the owner of the vessel demonstrates to the Secretary that—
the regional fishery management council of jurisdiction established under section 302(a)(1) of the Magnuson-Stevens Fishery Conservation and Management Act (

children.tail: , and the Secretary of Commerce has approved, conservation and management measures in accordance with the American Fisheries Act (

children.text: Public Law 105–277, div. C, title II

children.tail: See References in Text note below.
to allow the vessel to be used in fisheries under the council’s authority; and

children.text: in the case of a vessel listed in paragraphs (1) through (20) of section 208(e) of the American Fisheries Act (title II of division C of

children.tail: et seq.), the vessel is neither participating in nor eligible to participate in the non-AFA trawl catcher processor subsector (as that term is defined under section 219(a)(7) of the Department of Commerce and Related Agencies Appropriations Act, 2005 (

children.text: is either a rebuilt vessel or replacement vessel under section 208(g) of the American Fisheries Act (title II of division C of
is eligible for a fishery endorsement under this section; and
in the case of a vessel listed in paragraphs (1) through (20) of section 208(e) of the American Fisheries Act (title II of division C of

children.text: the vessel is a fish tender vessel that is not engaged in the harvesting or processing of fish.

The Administrator of the Maritime Administration shall administer subsections (c) and (d) with respect to vessels 100 feet or greater in registered length. The owner of each such vessel shall file a statement of citizenship setting forth all relevant facts regarding vessel ownership and control with the Administrator on an annual basis to demonstrate compliance with those provisions.

Regulations to implement this subsection shall conform to the extent practicable with the regulations establishing the form of citizenship affidavit set forth in part 355 of title 46, Code of Federal Regulations, as in effect on

children.tail: , except that the form of the statement shall be written in a manner to allow the owner of the vessel to satisfy any annual renewal requirements for a certificate of documentation for the vessel and to comply with this subsection and subsections (c) and (d), and shall not be required to be notarized.

children.text: Transfers of ownership and control of vessels subject to subsection (c) or (d), which are 100 feet or greater in registered length, shall be rigorously scrutinized for violations of those provisions, with particular attention given to—
leases, charters, mortgages, financing, and similar arrangements;
the control of persons not eligible to own a vessel with a fishery endorsement under subsection (c) or (d), over the management, sales, financing, or other operations of an entity; and
contracts involving the purchase over extended periods of time of all, or substantially all, of the living marine resources harvested by a fishing vessel.

The Secretary shall establish reasonable and necessary requirements to demonstrate compliance with subsections (c) and (d), with respect to vessels measuring less than 100 feet in registered length, and shall seek to minimize the administrative burden on individuals who own and operate those vessels.
Vessels Purchased Through Fishing Capacity Reduction Program
A vessel purchased by the Secretary of Commerce through a fishing capacity reduction program under the Magnuson-Stevens Fishery Conservation and Management Act (

children.tail: See References in Text note below.
of the Interjurisdictional Fisheries Act of 1986 (
) is not eligible for a fishery endorsement, and any fishery endorsement issued for that vessel is invalid.

children.text: The Secretary shall revoke the fishery endorsement of any vessel subject to subsection (c) or (d) whose owner does not comply with those provisions.

Regulations to implement subsections (c) and (d) and sections 12151(c) and 31322(b) of this title shall prohibit impermissible transfers of ownership or control, specify any transactions that require prior approval of an implementing agency, identify transactions that do not require prior agency approval, and to the extent practicable, minimize disruptions to the commercial fishing industry, to the traditional financing arrangements of that industry, and to the opportunity to form fishery cooperatives.

In subsection (b)(1), the word “only” is omitted because section 12102(a), as revised by the bill, contains a general requirement for appropriate documentation to engage in any trade.

In subsection (c)(1), the word “entity” is substituted for “corporation, partnership, association, trust, joint venture, limited liability company, limited liability partnership, or any other entity” to eliminate unnecessary words.

children.text: Inapplicability of Subsection (d) to Certain Menhaden Fisheries

“The limitation on registered length contained in section 12102(c)(6) [now section 12113(d)] of title 46, United States Code, shall not apply to a vessel used solely in any menhaden fishery which is located in the Gulf of Mexico or along the Atlantic coast south of the area under the authority of the New England Fishery Management Council for so long as such vessel is used in such fishery.”
Exception to Build Requirement for Prior Vessels
Notwithstanding the requirements of section 12108(a)(2) and (3) [now section 12113(a)(2) and (3)] of title 46, United States Code, a fishery license may be issued to a vessel that before
was documented under chapter 121 of that title; and
was operated as a fish processing or fish tender vessel in the navigable waters of the United States or the exclusive economic zone;

was a fish tender or fish processing vessel contracted to be purchased by a citizen of the United States, if the purchase is shown by contract or similarly reliable evidence acceptable to the Secretary to have been made for the purpose of using the vessel as a fish tender or fish processing vessel in the fisheries;
was documented under chapter 121 of that title and—
was rebuilt in a foreign country; or
is subsequently rebuilt in the United States for use as a fish processing vessel; or
was built in the United States and—
is rebuilt in a foreign country under a contract entered into before 6 months after the date of enactment of this Act [

children.tail: ], and was purchased or contracted to be purchased before
with the intent that the vessel be used in the fisheries, if that intent is evidenced by—

children.text: a ruling letter by the Coast Guard before

children.tail: under 46 C.F.R. § 67.21–1 or § 67.27–3 pursuant to a ruling request evidencing that intent; or

children.text: is purchased for use as a fish processing vessel under a contract entered into after
a contract to rebuild the vessel for use as a fish processing vessel was entered into before
that vessel is part of a specific business plan involving the conversion in foreign shipyards of a series of three vessels and rebuilding work on at least one of the vessels had begun before
A vessel rebuilt under subsection (a)(3)(B) or (4) of this section must be redelivered to the owner before

children.tail: . However, the Secretary may, on proof of circumstances beyond the control of the owner of a vessel affected by this section, extend the period for rebuilding in a foreign country permitted by this section.

children.text: Any fishery license or registry issued to a vessel built in a foreign country under this section shall be endorsed to restrict the vessel from catching, taking, or harvesting.
Before being issued a fishery license, any vessel described in subsection (a)(2) of this section must be documented under an application for documentation acceptable to the Secretary filed before

children.tail: , except that an alternative vessel of no greater tonnage than the vessel in the application may be substituted, if that substitution is made by the original applicant.”

children.text: “The terms in this Act [see Tables for classification] have the same meaning as in subtitle II [now also sections 114 and 115] of title 46, United States Code (as amended by this Act).”
